title: Yandex Predictor API keys should not be disclosed
description: In most cases, trust boundaries are violated when a secret is exposed in a source code repository or an uncontrolled deployment environment. Unintended people who don’t need to know the secret might get access to it. They might then be able to use it to gain unwanted access to associated services or resources.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Yandex Predictor API key detected. This key provides authenticated access to Yandex Machine Learning and Prediction services including data analysis, model training, predictions, and AI inference. Unauthorized access can lead to API quota abuse, computational resource theft, model tampering, and potential data mining of sensitive prediction requests. Immediately revoke this key from your Yandex Developer console and rotate credentials using secure environment variables.

Yandex Predictor API token detected. This token grants access to Yandex Machine Learning and prediction services with potentially extended permissions. Exposing Yandex ML tokens enables unauthorized model access, computational resource abuse, and potential manipulation of prediction algorithms for malicious purposes. Revoke immediately from your Yandex Developer account and implement secure credential storage.

Yandex Predictor client ID or model identifier detected. These identifiers are used to authenticate applications with Yandex Machine Learning and prediction services. While less sensitive than API keys, exposing client IDs can reveal ML model configurations, application architecture, and enable targeted attacks against your Yandex prediction integrations. Store securely using environment variables or configuration management.
